sections.paragraph:
-

(1) prop. like the Arab. قَوىَ to twist, to bind; whence قُوَّةُ a rope, Hebr. קָו and תִּקְוָה. Hence-

(2) to be strong, robust (for the notion of binding fast, tying fast, is applied to strength. See חוּל , חָזַק , No. 3 also the Germ. Stränge, i.e. ropes, ftrenge, and anftrengen, all of which are derived from the notion of binding fast). Hence קָו No. 2.

(3) to expect, to await (perhaps from enduring, remaining, which differs but little from the notion of strength; comp. חוּל No. 7), with acc. part. Kal קֹוֶה Psa 25:3 37:9 69:7 see Piel.

Piel קִוָּה i.q. Kal No. 3, to expect anything; followed by an acc. Job 30:26 אֶל Psa 27:14 37:34 לְ Jer 8:15 14:19 . Specially-(a) קִוָּה אֶת־יְהֹוָה Psa 25:5 39:8 40:2 ק׳ לַיהֹוָה Pro 20:22 אֶל יְיָ Psa 27:14 37:34 , to expect Jehovah, i.e. his aid, to fix one’s hope on him.-(b) to lie in wait for any one; followed by a dat. Psa 119:95 accus. (נֶפֶשׁ) Psa 56:7.

Niphal, to be gathered together (prop. mutually to expect one another, see Piel; or as others take it, to be wound together; See Kal No. I), used of nations, Jer 3:17 of waters, Gen 1:9.

Derivatives, קָו, מִקְוֶה, מִקְוָה, תִּקְוָה, and-
